source.title: Effort-based decision-making in major depressive disorder: a translational model of motivational anhedonia

confidence_rationale: Quantified clinical pin on the exact effort-cost trigger: MDD patients are less willing to expend effort for reward AND less able to integrate magnitude/probability into the choice; the over-disengaged phenotype deepens with episode duration. Confirms the trigger 'cannot simply be effort-cost or repeated-failure' and must be conditioned on value-integration integrity. Modest-N single-task clinical case-control.
failure_signatures: The agent systematically declines high-value goals whenever the effort estimate is high, even when expected reward clearly justifies the cost (shifted effort-cost trigger).
The agent fails to up-weight effort allocation as reward magnitude/probability rises -- the trigger ignores the value evidence that should suppress abandonment.
The over-disengagement phenotype intensifies with cumulative duration of low-reward/low-control experience (state-dependent drift of the trigger toward premature give-up).

mapping.source_claim_statement: MDD patients show reduced willingness to expend effort for reward on the EEfRT and impaired integration of reward magnitude and probability into effort allocation; longer current-episode duration predicts worse effort allocation -- isolating motivational anhedonia to the effort-cost decision stage, not pleasure capacity.
mapping.ree_translation: Empirical clinical pin for the ARC-078 C3 lower bound: a mis-set effort-cost trigger ALONE produces premature give-up, and the deficit is dual (shifted cost weight AND degraded value-integration). A REE abandon trigger must be conditioned on value-integration integrity, not effort cost alone -- an effort-cost cutoff cannot distinguish a rationally cost-sensitive agent from a pathologically disengaged one.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Clinical case-control, modest N, single task (EEfRT); the dual deficit (cost weight + value-integration) means a scalar-effort-threshold model cannot capture the distinction the data require. Corroborated by Treadway et al. 2009 PLoS One (PMID 19672310) and Treadway & Zald 2013 Curr Dir Psychol Sci (PMID 24748727).
mapping.source_context: Human case-control: MDD patients vs healthy controls; Effort Expenditure for Rewards Task (EEfRT); reward magnitude x probability manipulation.

confidence_components.notes: Clinical case-control, modest N, single task -- triangulated by the 2009 validation + 2013 review (medium-high source quality). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: it pins the exact trigger threshold and the value-integration condition. Transfer risk moderate.
